speaker: host
text: You said public markets would corrode the mission, then pitched them as discipline.

speaker: guest
text: Context changes and so does language.

speaker: host
text: That sounds polished but not explanatory. What changed concretely?

speaker: guest
text: We were responding to a very dynamic environment.

speaker: host
text: Did investors change your convictions or just your wording?

speaker: guest
text: I do not think that binary captures what was happening.

speaker: host
text: Then take the Nevada restructuring where people heard the news through a leaked deck.

speaker: guest
text: There are a lot of narratives about that period.

speaker: host
text: I am asking about your narrative, not theirs.

speaker: guest
text: My narrative is that leadership carries impossible tradeoffs.

speaker: host
text: Who exactly knew before the plant teams did?

speaker: guest
text: I am not going to reduce a complex chain of communication to one line item.

speaker: host
text: You keep describing complexity when I am asking about accountability.

speaker: guest
text: Accountability includes refusing simplistic theater after the fact.

speaker: host
text: Was the board told before employees yes or no?

speaker: guest
text: Yes, and I regret what that meant for trust.
